entries.summary: npm package manifest for sdd-forge, declaring its entry point, published files, Node.js engine requirement, and test scripts.
entries.detail: Defines the package as an ES module with a single CLI binary at src/sdd-forge.js. Published files are restricted to src/ while excluding preset test directories. Declares no runtime dependencies, relying solely on Node.js built-ins. Test scripts delegate to a custom test runner supporting unit, e2e, and acceptance scopes. Uses pnpm as the package manager and requires Node.js 18+.

entries.summary: Top-level dispatcher for the `sdd-forge docs` subcommand, routing CLI arguments to individual doc pipeline commands.
entries.detail: Parses the first positional argument to determine which docs subcommand to invoke (scan, enrich, init, data, text, readme, agents, translate, forge, review, changelog). Dynamically imports each command module at runtime to avoid loading unused code. Handles multi-language workflows by iterating over configured target languages and invoking init, data, and readme commands per language. Also proxies arbitrary script execution for internal tooling paths. Exits with error codes on unknown subcommands or pipeline failures.

entries.summary: Generates and refines AGENTS.md for a project by combining SDD template content with AI-analyzed project context.
entries.detail: Reads the existing AGENTS.md (creating a skeleton if absent) and resolves {{data}} directives within it using analysis.json. Constructs a structured AI prompt that includes docs chapter content, README, and package.json scripts to guide the agent in writing accurate PROJECT section content. Separates SDD-managed sections from project-specific content and replaces only the PROJECT block with the AI-refined output. Writes the updated AGENTS.md back to disk. Supports --sdd, --project, and --dry-run flags.

entries.summary: Generates a change_log.md document by parsing spec directories and extracting structured metadata from each spec.md file.
entries.detail: Scans the specs/ directory for numbered subdirectories, reading spec.md from each and extracting title, status, and date fields. Sanitizes text to remove HTML and escape special markdown characters. Produces a table-formatted changelog sorted by spec directory name. Outputs to docs/change_log.md by default or a path specified via --out. Uses a simple text-only approach with no AI involvement.

entries.summary: Fills {{data}} directives in docs chapter files by resolving them against the current analysis.json using a preset-aware resolver.
entries.detail: Loads analysis.json from the .sdd-forge/output directory and applies docsExclude filtering before passing it to createResolver(). Iterates over all chapter files in docs/, reads each template, and calls resolveDataDirectives() to substitute {{data(...)}} calls with tabular output from DataSource methods. Tracks which files changed and writes them back to disk. Exports populateFromAnalysis() for programmatic use by forge.js. Supports a FILE_CONTEXT_RULES mechanism to pass per-file contextual labels to resolvers.

entries.summary: AI-powered enrichment stage that annotates each analysis.json entry with summary, detail, chapter assignment, role, and keywords using batch AI calls.
entries.detail: Collects all entries from analysis.json across categories and splits them into token-bounded batches. For each batch, builds a structured prompt embedding the chapter list, entry file paths, and code snippets, then calls an AI agent asynchronously with configurable concurrency. Parses the JSON response and merges enrichment fields (summary, detail, chapter, role, keywords) back into the analysis object. Saves progress incrementally to avoid data loss on partial runs. Supports --reset and --dump flags for debugging failed responses.

entries.summary: Orchestrates multi-round AI-driven doc generation in local, assist, or agent modes, iterating until review passes or max runs is reached.
entries.detail: Resolves target doc files from chapter configuration and estimates relevant files from a spec's text. Builds system and per-file prompts using forge-prompts.js and invokes AI agents per file with configurable concurrency and timeout. After each round, runs the review command to check doc quality; if it fails, feeds the review output back as context for the next round. Supports an optional spec file to scope generation. Runs readme and translate commands as a final step after all rounds succeed. Exits with an error if max rounds are exhausted.

entries.summary: Initializes docs/ chapter files from preset templates, merging base and project-local templates and optionally using AI to filter relevant chapters.
entries.detail: Resolves templates by walking the preset inheritance chain and merging block overrides. Translates merged template content to the target language using translateTemplate(). Optionally calls aiFilterChapters() to remove chapters irrelevant to the project based on analysis.json. Writes chapter files to docs/, skipping existing files unless --force is passed. Supports --dry-run to preview without writing. Validates that at least one template is found before proceeding.

entries.summary: Generates or updates README.md by assembling a merged template, resolving {{data}} directives, and filling {{text}} blocks via AI.
entries.detail: Resolves the README template from the preset chain, applies data directives using a resolver aware of the readme output path context, and then invokes processTemplate() from text.js to fill {{text}} blocks with AI-generated content. Compares the resulting content against the existing README to skip unnecessary writes. Supports outputting to a custom path (e.g., docs/ja/README.md for language variants). Uses concurrency and agent configuration from config.json.

entries.summary: Validates the integrity and quality of generated docs chapter files, checking for exposed directives, minimum content length, and analysis coverage.
entries.detail: Checks each chapter file for residual block directives, unclosed HTML comments, and unfilled {{data}} or {{text}} placeholders that should have been replaced. Enforces a minimum line count per file to detect empty or truncated output. Cross-references analysis.json category-to-chapter mappings to ensure all categories are covered by at least one chapter. Validates translated language directories when configured. Reports all failures with structured messages and throws on any violation.

entries.summary: Scans source files using configured DataSources to build or incrementally update analysis.json with structured entry data per category.
entries.detail: Resolves the preset chain to collect DataSource definitions and loads them from each preset's data/ directory. For each DataSource, calls scan() to produce category entries, preserving existing IDs via a match-key index to enable stable incremental updates. Computes MD5 hashes of source files and DataSource files to detect changes. Supports --reset to clear specific categories. Outputs analysis.json to .sdd-forge/output/ and optionally prints it to stdout with --stdout.

entries.summary: Fills {{text}} directives in docs chapter files using AI agents, supporting both per-directive and batch-JSON response modes.
entries.detail: Parses each chapter file for {{text(...)}} directives and builds context-aware prompts embedding enriched analysis summaries and relevant source snippets. Sends all directives in a single batch request per file and parses the JSON response to apply each fill atomically. Validates results against shrinkage thresholds and required content rules before writing. Supports retry logic, dry-run mode, and change-detection to skip chapters whose source files have not been modified. Exports textFillFromAnalysis() for programmatic use by forge.js.

entries.summary: Translates docs chapter files and README.md to configured target languages using AI agents, skipping files that are already up to date.
entries.detail: Builds a list of translation tasks by comparing source mtime against target mtime for each (file, language) pair. Uses mapWithConcurrency to run translations in parallel against a configurable concurrency limit. Constructs a translation prompt with tone instructions derived from docs.style configuration. Strips AI response preamble before writing the translated content. Creates target language subdirectories as needed and supports --force to bypass freshness checks.

entries.summary: DataSource that provides AGENTS.md template content and project metadata fields such as lang, config, and package.json.
entries.detail: Reads the SDD AGENTS.md template from the base preset's template directory for the configured language. Exposes the project's config.json and package.json as structured data for use in {{data}} directives within AGENTS.md. Formats the SDD template content with a timestamp comment block for transparency. Designed to supply the agents command with the information needed to scaffold and update AGENTS.md.

entries.summary: DataSource providing docs navigation tables, language-relative file links, and chapter listing for use in {{data}} directives.
entries.detail: Reads docs chapter files to build a navigation table with titles, descriptions, and relative links. Computes cross-language relative paths between docs files depending on whether the current file is inside a language subdirectory. Provides a next/prev navigation footer based on chapter order. Loads language display names from locale/*/ui.json. Exposes repo URL from package.json for generating GitHub blob links to source files.

entries.summary: DataSource for resolving language-relative file paths and building language switch links used in multilingual docs.
entries.detail: Provides path resolution logic that translates a file path from the default language into its equivalent path within a target language subdirectory. Builds a formatted language switcher string with emoji globe icon and pipe-separated links. Reads config.json to determine the project's default language. Mirrors path logic from docs.js but as a standalone DataSource focused on language navigation.

entries.summary: DataSource that exposes project identity fields (name, description, version) sourced from package.json.
entries.detail: Caches the parsed package.json on first access to avoid repeated filesystem reads. Falls back to the directory basename if no name field is present. Returns a formatted markdown table of project metadata rows for use in {{data}} directives. Designed as a thin, read-only wrapper over package.json with no transformation beyond table formatting.

entries.summary: Minimal DataSource stub for the text category that returns null for all queries.
entries.detail: Serves as a placeholder DataSource registered under the 'text' category. All method calls return null, meaning no {{data}} directives of type text are resolved through this source. Its presence ensures the category is recognized by the DataSource loading infrastructure without contributing actual content.

entries.summary: Defines the AnalysisEntry base class, reserved meta key constants, and utility functions for entry validation and category summarization.
entries.detail: Exports ANALYSIS_META_KEYS as a Set of top-level analysis.json keys (analyzedAt, enrichedAt, generatedAt) that are not entry categories. AnalysisEntry provides a static fromObject() factory for deserialization. isEmptyEntry() checks whether an entry contains only the standard infrastructure keys (file, hash, lines, mtime). buildSummary() aggregates per-category entry arrays into a compact summary structure for prompt construction.

entries.summary: Filters analysis.json entries by applying docsExclude glob patterns configured in config.json.
entries.detail: Converts each docsExclude pattern to a regex via globToRegex() and removes entries whose file field matches any pattern. Provides two exports: filterByDocsExclude for flat entry arrays and filterAnalysisByDocsExclude for the full analysis object (iterating over all categories). Used by enrich.js and data.js to prevent excluded files from influencing doc generation.

entries.summary: Resolves the mapping between data categories in analysis.json and their assigned docs chapters by reading {{data}} directives from chapter files.
entries.detail: Parses each chapter file's directives to extract the data source categories referenced by {{data(...)}} calls, building a category-to-chapter map. mergeChapters() combines preset chapter definitions with project-specific overrides from config.json. extractDataCategories() identifies all categories declared within a single template string. Used by enrich.js to provide chapter context during AI enrichment prompts.

entries.summary: Resolves and packages the shared context object consumed by all docs commands, including root paths, config, agent, and chapter file list.
entries.detail: Reads config.json via sddConfigPath, validates it with validateConfig(), and resolves the agent from the agent configuration. Constructs the context with resolved root, docsDir, sourceRoot, lang, type, config, and agent fields. loadFullAnalysis() and loadAnalysisData() are convenience wrappers that read analysis.json from the output directory. getChapterFiles() lists chapter markdown files in a docs directory, filtering out reserved filenames and respecting configChapters ordering. stripResponsePreamble() removes boilerplate header lines from AI responses.

entries.summary: Provides a mapWithConcurrency utility that processes an array of items with a bounded number of concurrent async workers.
entries.detail: Implements a sliding-window concurrency model: starts up to `concurrency` workers simultaneously and launches the next worker as each one completes. Collects results in original order using index-based assignment. Returns a Promise that resolves to the full results array once all items are processed. Used throughout the docs pipeline (enrich, forge, translate, text) to parallelize AI agent calls without overwhelming the system.

entries.summary: Dynamically loads DataSource class instances from .js files in a given directory, instantiating each with root and docsDir context.
entries.detail: Reads all .js files from a directory and dynamically imports each module, expecting a default export that is a DataSource subclass. Instantiates each class and attaches _sourceFilePath for hash-based change detection in scan.js. Merges the newly loaded sources into an accumulator map keyed by source name (filename without extension). Used by scan.js to collect DataSources from each preset in the inheritance chain.

entries.summary: Base class for all DataSource implementations, providing desc(), toMarkdownTable(), and override-aware mapping helpers.
entries.detail: The desc() method delegates to _desc() for subclass-provided descriptions, returning an em-dash fallback. toMarkdownTable() formats a header row, separator, and body rows as a GitHub-flavored markdown table. withOverrides() merges per-entry summary overrides loaded from _loadOverrides() into an entry array, allowing project-specific customization without subclass modification. Subclasses override scan(), _desc(), and optionally _loadOverrides().

entries.summary: Parses and resolves {{data(...)}} and {{text(...)}} template directives embedded as HTML comments in markdown files.
entries.detail: Uses regex patterns to locate directive open/close comment pairs and extract their arguments (preset.source.method path, option params, labels). parseDirectives() returns a flat list of all directive objects with their line ranges for targeted replacement. resolveDataDirectives() iterates directives and calls a resolver function, replacing block or inline directive content in-place. stripBlockDirectives() removes template inheritance markers ({%block%}, {%extends%}). parseBlocks() extracts named block content for template merging.

entries.summary: Builds structured AI prompts for the forge command, including system prompts and per-file or full-doc prompts with analysis context.
entries.detail: summaryToText() converts analysis.json into a human-readable summary string by delegating to enrichedSummaryToText(), which formats each category's entries with their enriched summary and detail fields. buildForgeSystemPrompt() constructs the system-level instruction block with language, user intent, optional spec content, and analysis summary. buildForgeFilePrompt() and buildForgePrompt() produce the per-file and full-document prompt variants respectively, embedding round number, max runs, and prior review feedback for iterative improvement.

entries.summary: Factory module that maps file extensions to language-specific handler objects for JS, PHP, Python, and YAML files.
entries.detail: Maintains an EXT_MAP dictionary keyed by lowercase file extension. The getLangHandler() function extracts the extension from a file path and returns the matching handler or null. Acts as a central dispatch point for all language-aware operations in the docs pipeline.

entries.summary: JavaScript language handler providing parse, minify, extractImports, extractExports, and extractEssential operations for JS source files.
entries.detail: Implements comment stripping (block and line), indent normalization, and AST-like structural parsing to extract class name, parent class, methods, and properties from JS files. extractEssential filters tokens to retain only those involving core Node.js APIs. Uses regex-based parsing rather than a full AST parser to stay dependency-free.

entries.summary: PHP language handler providing parse, minify, extractImports, extractExports, and extractEssential operations analogous to the JS handler.
entries.detail: Extends comment-stripping to include PHP hash-style comments in addition to block and line comments. Parses PHP class name, parent class, methods, properties, and relations via regex. Mirrors the JS handler interface so both can be used interchangeably through the lang-factory dispatch.

entries.summary: Python language handler providing minify (hash comment removal) and extractEssential for Python source files.
entries.detail: Implements only hash comment stripping since Python does not have block comment syntax. extractEssential filters lines to retain semantically significant content. Intentionally minimal compared to JS/PHP handlers because Python parsing requirements in the pipeline are simpler.

entries.summary: YAML language handler providing minify via hash comment removal for YAML source files.
entries.detail: Contains only a removeHashComments helper and a minify export; no parse or extractEssential since YAML files are not structurally parsed in the pipeline. Follows the same interface contract as other language handlers so lang-factory can dispatch uniformly.

entries.summary: Unified minify facade that delegates to per-language handlers and applies common post-processing such as blank line and trailing whitespace removal.
entries.detail: Resolves the appropriate handler via getLangHandler, then calls extractEssential or the handler's own minify depending on the opts flag. Falls back gracefully when no handler is found. Centralizes blank-line collapsing and trailing-whitespace stripping so individual language handlers need not repeat that logic.

entries.summary: Utility library for parsing PHP array syntax: extracting array bodies, top-level keys, quoted strings, and performing camelCase-to-snake_case conversion.
entries.detail: stripBlockComments removes C-style comments before extraction. extractArrayBody locates a named property and returns its raw array content. extractTopLevelKeys and extractQuotedStrings use regex to pull identifiers and string literals. camelToSnake and pluralize are naming helpers used by PHP-aware DataSources.

entries.summary: Factory that constructs resolver instances combining preset-chain DataSources, project overrides, and a desc() helper for documentation generation.
entries.detail: loadDataSources wraps the base loader with project-specific context. loadOverridesFor reads overrides.json from the .sdd-forge directory with a module-level cache. descFactory builds a closure returning override values or a dash fallback. createResolver assembles the full resolver object exposing methods like resolve(), list(), and direct DataSource method dispatch.

entries.summary: Parser utilities for extracting structured data from flow review output text: Q&A questions, failure summaries, and per-file pass/fail results.
entries.detail: extractNeedsInput scans output lines for question markers and returns non-empty items. summarizeReview picks the first failure block or returns a placeholder. parseFileResults matches output file base names against a provided allFiles list to classify outcomes. Used downstream to drive re-review loops and report generation.

entries.summary: Scannable mixin that extends a base class with scan-lifecycle stubs (skip predicate, entry factory).
entries.detail: Exports a single higher-order function Scannable(Base) that returns a class extending Base. Provides a default return-false skip method and a newEntry() factory that constructs a new Entry. Acts as a composable building block for DataSource classes that need scanning behavior.

entries.summary: Core file scanning utilities providing pattern matching, file stats collection, directory traversal, and language-aware file parsing.
entries.detail: patternToRegex and globToRegex convert glob patterns to RegExp. findFiles and collectFiles walk directory trees respecting include/exclude lists, returning sorted relative paths. parsePHPFile and parseJSFile delegate to language handlers while parseFile dispatches generically. Re-exports camelToSnake and pluralize from php-array-parser for convenience.

entries.summary: Template merging engine that resolves documentation templates from preset inheritance chains and merges additive or override blocks.
entries.detail: buildLayers constructs an ordered list of template directories from the preset chain. resolveOneFile picks the first matching file per layer. mergeResolved handles both additive (append new blocks) and override (child wins) merge strategies. resolveTemplates orchestrates multi-chain resolution for all chapter files. translateTemplate calls an AI agent to produce language-translated template content. SPECIAL_FILES are excluded from normal chapter merging.

entries.summary: Detects the test environment and frameworks present in a project from its analysis data.
entries.detail: Iterates analysis entries looking for known test framework indicators defined in TEST_FRAMEWORKS. Returns a result object with hasTestEnv boolean, matched framework names, and a suggested testCommand string. Used during flow review to tailor test design prompts.

entries.summary: Assembles AI prompts for the text generation pipeline, including system prompts, per-directive prompts, batch prompts, and enriched context payloads.
entries.detail: CATEGORY_TO_SECTIONS maps analysis categories to relevant chapter sections. getAnalysisContext collects data fields for a directive while getEnrichedContext adds minified source code up to MAX_CHARS. buildTextSystemPrompt and buildFileSystemPrompt compose multi-part system prompt strings with i18n support. buildBatchPrompt formats multiple text directives into a single numbered request list for bulk AI calls.

entries.summary: Minimal TOML parser supporting key-value pairs, string literals, and arrays for config file parsing.
entries.detail: parseTOML iterates lines, splits on the first '=', and delegates value parsing to parseValue. parseValue detects quoted strings and arrays. parseArray handles comma-separated items by stripping brackets and splitting. Only supports the TOML subset needed for sdd-forge configuration files.

entries.summary: Top-level flow command dispatcher that parses CLI arguments, resolves project context, and routes to registered flow subcommands.
entries.detail: Reads process.argv to identify the command group and subcommand, then calls resolveCtx() which loads config, flow state, and determines worktree vs main repo paths. runEntry() invokes the target command's execute() function and handles errors. dispatch() applies exit codes from flow envelope results. Uses FLOW_COMMANDS registry for all routing decisions.

entries.summary: Implements the flow merge step, choosing between PR creation via GitHub CLI and squash merge based on config and tool availability.
entries.detail: resolveRemote reads push.remote from config. isGhAvailable probes the gh binary. parseSpec extracts title and body sections from spec.md markdown. buildPrTitle and buildPrBody compose PR metadata from spec and flow state. main() returns a strategy object ('skip', 'pr', or 'squash') which the caller uses to drive actual git/gh operations.

entries.summary: Generates and persists flow execution reports aggregating phase metrics and redo counts into a JSON/text report.
entries.detail: aggregateMetrics sums per-phase counters (question, redo, docsRead, srcRead) from the flow state metrics array. generateReport produces both a structured data object and a human-readable text summary. saveReport writes report.json into the spec directory alongside other flow artifacts.

entries.summary: Full AI-driven code review pipeline supporting draft proposal review, final verdict application, and test design gap analysis with iterative fix loops.
entries.detail: resolveReviewTarget assembles spec text and changed file contents. buildDraftSystemPrompt and buildFinalSystemPrompt define review personas. parseProposals and mergeVerdicts extract structured review results. runTestReview orchestrates test design generation, gap analysis via AI, and automated test file writing through applyTestFixes. Supports up to MAX_TEST_REVIEW_RETRIES iterations to close remaining gaps.

entries.summary: Checks prerequisites for flow step transitions, validating git cleanliness and GitHub CLI availability.
entries.detail: VALID_TARGETS defines accepted check targets. PREREQS maps each target to required prior steps. checkStepPrereqs compares required steps against flow state completions. checkDirty runs git status to detect uncommitted changes. checkGh probes gh availability. execute() dispatches to the appropriate check function and returns a structured ok/fail envelope.

entries.summary: Retrieves flow context including raw file contents, analysis entries, and search results over the project's analysis.json.
entries.detail: Supports three modes: file content retrieval, full analysis dump, and keyword/ngram/AI-powered search. contextSearch dispatches to aiSearch or ngramSearch depending on mode. ngramSearch uses bigram similarity scoring. aiSearch uses an AI agent to select relevant keywords then falls back to fallbackSearch on error. EXCLUDE_FIELDS prevents internal metadata from leaking into search results.

entries.summary: Retrieves guardrail rules for a specified flow phase and formats them as JSON or Markdown.
entries.detail: parseGuardrailArgs extracts a positional phase argument and optional --json flag. Calls loadMergedGuardrails and filterByPhase from the guardrail library. toMarkdown converts guardrail entries to a bulleted Markdown list. Returns a structured output envelope.

entries.summary: Fetches a GitHub issue by number using the gh CLI and returns its content as a parsed JSON envelope.
entries.detail: Executes gh issue view with --json flag and parses stdout. Wraps the result in the standard ok/fail envelope. Minimal implementation with no caching; intended for inline spec enrichment during flow planning.

entries.summary: Returns pre-built prompt strings indexed by language and kind for use in flow planning and spec writing.
entries.detail: PROMPTS_BY_LANG is a static lookup table keyed by language code (e.g. 'ja') and prompt kind. validKinds() enumerates available kinds. execute() validates the requested language and kind before returning the prompt string in the output envelope.

entries.summary: Returns the current QA item count from the active flow state.
entries.detail: Reads the flow state and counts qa entries. Returns the count in an ok envelope. Used by skills to decide whether to run a QA phase or skip it.

entries.summary: Resolves full execution context for skill-level orchestration: worktree path, branch, ahead count, last commit, spec content, and active flow metadata.
entries.detail: Handles both worktree and main-repo scenarios by probing worktree paths and falling back to the main repo. extractSection parses markdown headings from spec text to surface requirements and acceptance criteria. Reads flow.json by specId or by scanning for the active flow. Returns a comprehensive context envelope used by orchestration skills.

entries.summary: Returns the current flow phase derived from step completion status.
entries.detail: Calls derivePhase() from flow-state with the current steps array. Returns the phase string (draft, spec, gate, impl, etc.) in a structured output envelope. Used by skills to determine the current position in the SDD workflow.

entries.summary: Central registry mapping all flow command paths to their handler modules, with pre/post hooks for step status tracking and metric recording.
entries.detail: FLOW_COMMANDS is a nested object keyed by group (run, get, set) and subcommand. stepPre sets a step to 'in_progress' before execution; stepPost updates it to 'done' or 'failed' based on the envelope result. deriveActivePhase reads the current phase from flow state for context-aware dispatching. The registry pattern decouples flow.js dispatch from individual command implementations.

entries.summary: Implements the finalize step: merges or creates a PR, runs docs build, and cleans up the worktree.
entries.detail: STEP_MAP defines the finalize sub-steps. executeCleanup removes the worktree via git worktree remove after squash or PR merge. Invokes docs.js as a child process for documentation sync. generateReport and saveReport persist the final flow report. Handles spec-only mode (no git operations) as a special case.

entries.summary: Validates a spec file against structural rules and AI-evaluated guardrails before allowing the flow to advance to implementation.
entries.detail: checkSpecText enforces structural requirements (section presence, length minimums) while skipping pre-defined sections like Status and Acceptance Criteria. buildGuardrailPrompt formats applicable guardrail rules into an AI prompt. parseGuardrailResponse extracts pass/fail verdicts from AI output. checkGuardrail orchestrates the AI call and aggregates results into a passed boolean.

entries.summary: Confirms implementation readiness by summarizing changed files and requirement completion status from the spec.
entries.detail: getChangedFiles uses git diff against the base branch to list modified files. summarizeRequirements counts total, done, pending, and in-progress items parsed from spec text. execute() checks spec file existence and populates the output envelope with both the file list and requirements summary for skill-level display.

entries.summary: Runs lint checks by loading merged guardrails and invoking the lint runner against the current codebase.
entries.detail: parseArgs extracts optional flags. loadMergedGuardrails fetches the combined ruleset for the project. runLint from lib/lint.js performs the actual check. Returns an ok/fail envelope based on lint results. Used as a pre-merge quality gate in the flow.

entries.summary: Prepares the spec directory, creates spec.md and qa.md from templates, initializes flow state, and optionally sets up a git worktree for the new flow.
entries.detail: nextIndex scans the specs/ directory to assign a sequential spec number. slugify converts the request text into a branch-safe name. ensureClean guards against dirty working trees. detectBaseBranch identifies the current branch as the merge target. createSpecTemplate and createQaTemplate support local template overrides in .sdd-forge/templates/. saveFlowState, addActiveFlow, and cleanStaleFlows maintain the flow registry JSON.

entries.summary: Runner wrapper that generates and saves a flow report from the current flow state and redo log.
entries.detail: Loads flow state and redo log, calls generateReport from the shared report module, and persists the result via saveReport. Returns an ok envelope with the path of the changed report.json. Thin wrapper delegating all logic to flow/commands/report.js.

entries.summary: Runs an AI-driven retrospective that evaluates whether the implementation fulfilled each requirement by analyzing the git diff.
entries.detail: extractRequirements parses the requirements section from spec.md. getDiff and getDetailedDiff run git diff against baseBranch. buildRetroPrompt formats requirements and diff into a structured AI prompt. parseRetroResponse extracts per-requirement verdicts from the AI JSON response. Writes retro.json to the spec directory; --force flag allows overwriting an existing retro.

entries.summary: Runner that invokes the review command script as a subprocess and parses its structured output.
entries.detail: Builds the script path using PKG_DIR and delegates execution to runSync. parseTestReviewOutput interprets stdout/stderr and exit codes to construct an ok/fail envelope. Acts as a thin subprocess wrapper so the registry can treat review as a standard flow step.

entries.summary: Runs the docs build script as a subprocess to sync documentation with the current source code.
entries.detail: Resolves the docs.js script path via PKG_DIR and invokes it through runSync with forwarded arguments. Returns ok/fail based on exit code. Used in finalize and explicit sync steps to keep documentation up to date after implementation.

entries.summary: Sets or clears the auto-approve flag in the active flow state.
entries.detail: Calls mutateFlowState to toggle the auto field based on the subcommand argument (on/off). Returns an ok envelope confirming the new state. Used by orchestration skills to suppress per-step user confirmation prompts.

entries.summary: Associates a GitHub issue number with the active flow state.
entries.detail: Delegates to setIssue() from flow-state, writing the issue reference into flow.json. Returns a structured ok/fail envelope. Enables traceability between flow work and the originating GitHub issue.

entries.summary: Records phase-level metric counters (question, redo, docsRead, srcRead) into the active flow state.
entries.detail: VALID_PHASES and VALID_COUNTERS define the accepted inputs. Calls mutateFlowState to increment the specified counter within the current phase's metrics entry. Used by skills to track quality signals across the SDD workflow for retrospective reporting.

entries.summary: Appends a timestamped note to the active flow state's notes array.
entries.detail: Delegates to addNote() from flow-state which mutates flow.json in place. Returns an ok envelope. Notes provide a lightweight audit trail within a flow for decisions and observations recorded by skills or the user.

entries.summary: Manages the redo log (redolog.json) that tracks implementation retry history within a spec directory.
entries.detail: loadRedoLog reads and validates redolog.json, returning an empty entries array if the file is absent or newly created. saveRedoLog writes the log atomically with pretty-print formatting. execute() appends a new redo entry with reason and timestamp. Re-exports loadRedoLog and saveRedoLog for use by report.js.

entries.summary: Updates the status of an individual requirement item in the active flow state.
entries.detail: Calls updateRequirement() from flow-state with the requirement ID and new status value. Returns an ok/fail envelope. Used by skills to mark requirements as done or in-progress during implementation.

entries.summary: Sets the original user request text in the active flow state.
entries.detail: Delegates to setRequest() from flow-state, persisting the raw request string that initiated the flow. Returns a structured envelope. Provides traceability back to the user's original feature request throughout the SDD lifecycle.

entries.summary: Updates the status of a named step in the active flow state.
entries.detail: Calls updateStepStatus() from flow-state with the step ID and status string. Returns a structured envelope. Used programmatically by registry hooks and directly by skills to advance or roll back step states.

entries.summary: Sets the structured requirements list in flow state by parsing a JSON string argument.
entries.detail: Attempts JSON.parse on the raw argument before calling setRequirements() from flow-state. Returns an ok envelope on success or a fail envelope with a PARSE_ERROR code if the input is not valid JSON. Used after AI-generated requirement extraction during the spec phase.

entries.summary: Sets the test summary (unit/integration/acceptance results) in the active flow state.
entries.detail: TYPE_FLAGS defines the three accepted test type flags. parseArgs extracts the type and summary text from CLI arguments. Calls setTestSummary() from flow-state. Returns a structured envelope. Used after the test review step to record test coverage outcomes.

entries.summary: Help command entry point that formats and displays the list of available CLI commands with translated descriptions.
entries.detail: LAYOUT defines the ordered list of command groups and entries. main() maps each entry to a {name, desc} object using i18n translations keyed as ui:help.commands.<name>. Exports both main and the LAYOUT array so other modules can introspect available commands.

entries.summary: Core AI agent invocation library providing synchronous, asynchronous, and retry-aware agent calls with stdin fallback for large prompts.
entries.detail: callAgent executes the configured agent binary synchronously via execFileSync. callAgentAsync and callAgentAsyncWithRetry implement async invocation with configurable retry count and delay. ARGV_SIZE_THRESHOLD detects prompts that would exceed OS ARG_MAX and routes them through stdin instead. resolveAgent merges provider profile args with base args and resolves the command. writeAgentContext and cleanupAgentContext manage temporary rules files in .claude/rules/ for context injection.

entries.summary: Loads the SDD section template (AGENTS.sdd.md) from the base preset for use in AGENTS.md generation.
entries.detail: Resolves the template path relative to PRESETS_DIR using the requested language with an 'en' fallback. Returns the file contents as a string or an empty string if not found. Used by the agents command to populate the SDD context block in AGENTS.md.

entries.summary: CLI utility module providing PKG_DIR constant, repoRoot/sourceRoot resolution, parseArgs, worktree detection, and package version retrieval.
entries.detail: repoRoot respects SDD_WORK_ROOT env var before falling back to git rev-parse. sourceRoot respects SDD_SOURCE_ROOT. parseArgs implements a spec-driven option parser supporting boolean and string flags with unknown-option errors. isInsideWorktree checks whether .git is a file (worktree) vs directory. getMainRepoPath resolves the main repo root from a worktree's gitdir commondir.

entries.summary: Configuration loading and path resolution utilities for the .sdd-forge project directory.
entries.detail: Defines path helpers sddDir, sddConfigPath, sddOutputDir, and sddDataDir anchored to the SDD_DIR_NAME constant. loadConfig reads and validates config.json via validateConfig from types.js. loadJsonFile is a generic JSON loader with existence checking. loadPackageField reads a named field from package.json. DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY and DEFAULT_LANG provide fallback values.

entries.summary: Utility for detecting direct script execution and conditionally running a main() function.
entries.detail: isDirectRun compares the resolved argv[1] path against the module's own file URL to detect top-level invocation. runIfDirect wraps main() calls with process.exit(EXIT_ERROR) on rejection. Used throughout the codebase as a standard guard to prevent side effects when a module is imported rather than executed directly.

entries.summary: Defines the two standard process exit code constants used throughout the codebase.
entries.detail: Exports EXIT_SUCCESS (0) and EXIT_ERROR (1) as named constants. Centralizing exit codes prevents magic numbers and ensures consistency across all command modules.

entries.summary: Factory functions for the standard flow command response envelope (ok, fail, warn) and the output() function that writes JSON to stdout and sets process.exitCode.
entries.detail: ok, fail, and warn each return a structured object with ok boolean, type, key, data, and errors fields. output() serializes the envelope as indented JSON to stdout and sets process.exitCode without calling process.exit(), allowing post-output hooks to run. This envelope format is the contract between all flow commands and their callers.

entries.summary: Manages persistent flow state for SDD workflows, tracking step progress, metrics, and active flows across worktrees via flow.json files.
entries.detail: Provides read/write operations for flow state stored in specs/<specId>/flow.json. Tracks step statuses through a defined FLOW_STEPS array with PHASE_MAP for phase derivation. Handles concurrent flows via .active-flow file listing multiple active spec IDs. Includes stale flow cleanup by checking worktree and branch existence. Exposes mutation helpers (updateStepStatus, incrementMetric, setRequirements, etc.) that all delegate to a central mutateFlowState function.

entries.summary: Thin utility module for reading git repository state including branch name, dirty status, ahead count, and last commit.
entries.detail: All functions wrap execFileSync via a tryExec helper that returns null on failure instead of throwing. getWorktreeStatus returns dirty flag plus list of dirty files from git status --porcelain. getAheadCount measures how many commits the current branch is ahead of a base branch. isGhAvailable probes for the gh CLI binary with a 5-second timeout.

entries.summary: Loads, merges, and matches guardrail rules from JSON files across preset chains and project-level overrides.
entries.detail: Guardrails are read from templates/<lang>/guardrail.json within each preset in the resolved chain, then merged by ID with project-level guardrail.json taking precedence. Each entry is hydrated with compiled lint regex via parseLintString. filterByPhase selects guardrails applicable to a given phase (defaulting to 'spec'). matchScope checks whether a file path matches a guardrail's scope patterns using patternToRegex from the scanner module.

entries.summary: Provides internationalization support by loading and merging locale JSON files from package, preset, and project directories.
entries.detail: createI18n builds a translation function t(key, params) that resolves dot-separated keys across ui/messages/prompts domains, with {{param}} interpolation. The translate() factory supports namespaced keys in 'domain:key' format. Locale directories are layered: package locale → preset locale → project locale, with later layers overriding earlier via deepMerge. extractPresetKey strips path prefixes for backwards compatibility with legacy type strings.

entries.summary: Resolves and expands <!-- include("path") --> directives in template files, with path sandboxing and circular-reference detection.
entries.detail: resolveIncludes scans content line-by-line for the INCLUDE_RE pattern and recursively replaces matches with the referenced file's content. Path resolution supports four strategies: templatesDir prefix, presetsDir prefix, pkgDir prefix, or baseDir-relative. Paths containing ../ or ./ are rejected to prevent directory traversal. A Set tracks resolved paths to detect and abort on circular includes.

entries.summary: Repairs malformed or truncated JSON strings produced by LLMs, making them parseable by JSON.parse.
entries.detail: repairJson implements a hand-written recursive descent parser that tolerates unclosed strings, missing commas, trailing commas, and incomplete structures by reconstructing valid JSON in an output buffer. stripMarkdownFences removes code block wrappers before parsing. findJsonStart skips leading prose to locate the first { or [ character. Utility predicates (isWhitespace, isDigit, escapeControl) handle character-level operations. The repair logic handles all JSON value types: objects, arrays, strings, numbers, and keywords.

entries.summary: Executes lint guardrail checks against changed source files, comparing file content against regex patterns defined in guardrail rules.
entries.detail: getChangedFiles uses git diff to enumerate files changed relative to a base branch. runLintChecks reads each changed file and tests its content against filtered lint guardrails via matchScope. runLint orchestrates the full pipeline: filters guardrails by phase, retrieves changed files, runs checks, and returns a structured result with ok flag, warnings, failure details, and counts. validateLintGuardrails pre-validates guardrail entries for required fields.

entries.summary: Renders an interactive terminal multi-select UI for choosing preset types, supporting both single and multi-selection modes.
entries.detail: buildTreeItems converts a flat preset list into indented tree items with visual prefix characters. select() returns a Promise and uses raw TTY mode to capture arrow keys, space, and enter. adjustViewport keeps the cursor-selected item visible within a scrollable window. selectAncestors automatically selects all parent nodes when a leaf is selected. Falls back to returning the first item non-interactively when stdin is not a TTY.

entries.summary: Discovers all available presets and resolves their inheritance chains by following parent references in preset.json manifests.
entries.detail: discoverPresets scans the presets/ directory for subdirectories containing a preset.json and builds a PRESETS array with key, parent, label, dir, and meta fields. resolveChain follows parent links to build an ordered array from root to leaf, detecting circular references and missing parents. resolveMultiChains deduplicates and merges chains for multi-type configurations. presetByLeaf and presetsForArch provide lookup helpers.

entries.summary: Minimal wrapper around Node.js spawnSync for executing shell commands synchronously and capturing stdout/stderr.
entries.detail: runSync accepts a command, argument array, and options object, forwarding them to spawnSync with stdio set to pipe by default. Returns an object with stdout, stderr, and status fields. Serves as the single abstraction point for subprocess execution used by lint and other modules that need synchronous process output.

entries.summary: Provides a multi-step progress display with spinner animation and elapsed time for long-running CLI pipeline operations.
entries.detail: createProgress accepts a steps array and renders a progress bar to stderr with step index, percentage, current label, and a braille spinner. In TTY mode it uses ANSI escape codes to update lines in place; in non-TTY mode it falls back to plain newline output. createLogger provides simple stderr prefix logging. formatElapsed formats milliseconds into h/m/s strings. A module-level _current reference tracks the active progress instance.

entries.summary: Deploys skill SKILL.md files from the package templates directory into .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/ in the project.
entries.detail: deploySkills reads skill directories under src/templates/skills/, resolves include directives via resolveIncludes to produce final content, then writes the result to both .agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Existing symlinks are replaced unconditionally; regular files are only overwritten when content has changed to avoid unnecessary disk writes. Returns a results array summarising deployed, skipped, and unchanged skills.

entries.summary: Validates config.json structure and resolves output configuration with defaults for language, purpose, and tone fields.
entries.detail: validateConfig checks required fields (lang, type, docs object with languages array and defaultLanguage) and validates docs.style.tone against the VALID_TONES set, collecting all errors before throwing. resolveOutputConfig merges user-supplied config with sensible defaults, normalising the docs output shape. Acts as the central schema enforcement point for all loaded configuration objects.

entries.summary: CLI entry point for the 'presets' subcommand, printing the available preset tree or details about a specific preset.
entries.detail: Reads process.argv[2] to dispatch between subcommands. printTree walks the PRESETS array and renders a visual hierarchy using printNode with Unicode tree characters. Each node shows whether a templates directory exists. Uses EXIT_ERROR for unknown subcommands. Relies entirely on the presets.js discovery module with no additional state.

entries.summary: Main entry point for the sdd-forge CLI, dispatching subcommands to namespace dispatchers (docs, flow) or standalone script modules.
entries.detail: Parses the first argument to determine the subcommand. NAMESPACE_DISPATCHERS (docs, flow) are loaded as dispatcher modules that handle further sub-routing. INDEPENDENT maps simple subcommands (setup, upgrade, presets, agents) to their script paths. For all cases, process.argv is mutated before dynamic import so that each module sees correct argv. Handles --help/-h/help as a special case loading help.js.

entries.summary: Interactive setup wizard that initialises a new sdd-forge project by collecting configuration, writing config.json, deploying skills, and generating AGENTS.md.
entries.detail: Prompts the user for project name, source path, language, preset type(s), docs purpose, and tone via readline, with defaults loaded from any existing config.json. Writes the validated config to .sdd-forge/config.json. Creates required directories (docs/, specs/, .sdd-forge/output/) and updates .gitignore and .gitattributes. Calls deploySkills to install skill files and ensureAgentConfigFile to create or update AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md with the SDD directive block. Supports --dry-run and --json flags.

entries.summary: Upgrade command that redeploys skill files and migrates config.json to the current schema without user interaction.
entries.detail: Resolves the project root via repoRoot, loads and validates the existing config with loadConfig, then calls deploySkills to overwrite outdated SKILL.md files. Writes back a normalised config.json if schema migration is needed. Accepts --dry-run to preview changes without writing. Exits with EXIT_ERROR on missing or invalid config.
